pattern: Answer queries offline from largest required size down, adding rooms into a sorted structure so each query becomes a nearest-id lookup by binary search.
hints: Process queries from the largest minimum size to the smallest so the set of eligible rooms only grows.
Keep the eligible room ids in sorted order; the best answer is one of the two neighbors of the preferred id.

Sort rooms by size descending and queries by required size descending; insert room ids into a sorted list as they qualify, then for each query binary-search for the id closest to preferred, breaking ties toward the smaller id.

edge_inputs.description: tie in distance must pick the smaller id
single room, single query that fits
single room too small for the query
no room large enough returns minus one
closest qualifying room is below preferred while a closer one is too small

stress.description: many random rooms and queries with unique room ids
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    ids = rng.sample(range(1, 10_000_001), min(n, 9_000_000))
    rooms = [[rid, rng.randrange(1, 1001)] for rid in ids]
    k = max(1, min(n // 2 + 1, 2000))
    queries = [[rng.randrange(1, 10_000_001), rng.randrange(1, 1001)] for _ in range(k)]
    return [rooms, queries]
stress.note: unique room ids via sample; sizes kept modest so many queries succeed

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def closestRoom(self, rooms, queries):
        rooms = sorted(rooms, key=lambda r: r[1])
        q = sorted(range(len(queries)), key=lambda i: queries[i][1])
        ans = [-1] * len(queries)
        ids = []
        idx = len(rooms) - 1
        for qi in reversed(q):
            pref, msz = queries[qi]
            while idx >= 0 and rooms[idx][1] >= msz:
                bisect.insort(ids, rooms[idx][0])
                idx -= 1
            if not ids:
                ans[qi] = -1
                continue
            pos = bisect.bisect_left(ids, pref)
            best = -1
            best_dist = None
            for p in (pos - 1, pos):
                if 0 <= p < len(ids):
                    rid = ids[p]
                    d = abs(rid - pref)
                    if best_dist is None or d < best_dist or (d == best_dist and rid < best):
                        best_dist = d
                        best = rid
            ans[qi] = best
        return ans
